Cool.

So, I think my goal for today, because 20 minutes, I mean, there's a lot of time, but also not a lot of time, is to kind of just, like, get everyone on the same page of what we're doing, kind of what the opportunity is, just so everyone's hopefully, like, going to eventually get, you know, comfortable with , like, talking about this, whether it's to a client or to, you know, the other people you see and work with.
And then we'll start to get into how we're actually delivering.
And then I'll leave.

I'll like, a few minutes at the end for questions, so, like, as I'm kind of going through this little deck that I prepared, just dump all your questions into the comments, and then at the end I'll try to go through as many as I can.
So, high-level, I think, you know, the truth of it is the way people kind of just, like, find information and make decisions around products is changing, like, dramatically.

You know, we're going from, like, a world where search kind of drove a lot of the organic traffic to now that moving to the AI apps, and there's a ton of AI apps, and they all work kind of slightly differences, and there's nuances in how to kind of get in there.
But, essentially, what we're trying to do as GrowthX is help our customers get mentioned and to shape that through being cited and being kind of trained in these models.

So, I mean, I'm sure you guys have seen all sorts of different stats, you know, I think, anecdotally, I've heard, you know, multiple of even our customers having, you know, traffic decline, at least from search.

You know, people have seen the rise of, like, referral traffic coming to their website and seeing that convert really, really well, all to kind of support this, which I think is the truth of, you know, like, the AI apps are driving more people, and people are more and more kind of going to them to just, like, learn, like, what products to buy and what to do.
So, like, what does that actually mean, especially relevant to us?
I think if Marcel hasn't shared it already, he kind of recorded this pretty, you know, insightful video on just the fundamentals mattering more.
And for us, the fundamentals are around, we understand the cutoff, we understand the the
And kind of their problems and use cases, and also, you know, the clients that we serve, and we use that to create helpful content that's valuable.
I think that's never more true than it is now.
think like early days of SEO, you could like game it by hiding keywords in the footer and doing, you know, shady things like that.
But I think the trend has always been towards like, you know, doing good content kind of just looks like being helpful.
And I think with AI, it feels more and more true now, where if you start looking at what shapes what an AI says about your product, it just kind of looks like good marketing, you know.
It looks like you know how to talk to your audience and be consistent and make sure, you know, the world knows who you are.
I think, you know, getting more tactical, the truth is everything is being more personalized.
You know, you guys hopefully are all using, you know, ChatGPT or Claude or these products, and like they over time learn more and more.
Bye

And you when you ask them questions and they go out to the internet to search, you can see that the searches are much more personalized and much more long tail than what a human would typically do and what you would typically see in SEMrush.
And I think what that means is, you know, everything's more long tail, so you kind of need more content to serve those long tail searches.
Like, you need to be a little bit less general and more targeted in the way you create content.
And that's great for us because, you know, we build machines that create a lot of quality content.
I think the demands for kind of quality and recency is higher.

So what that means is, you know, well, I mean, like, first, like, you know, where this comes from is if you look at how these AI kind of prioritize what information they use, like, it's really clear that they prefer things published recently.
do where we definitely want at it is split of our for And we with we seems

So what that means is you need to start kind of refreshing your content more.
So like, you know, in the past, I'm sure all of our clients have a blog post they wrote like three years ago that they haven't even touched.
I think going forward, you need to kind of treat your content almost like a product on its own.
And, you know, if you have a thousand articles, that whole kind of catalog of content needs to kind of iterate, you know, with what you learn about how AI uses content.
Yeah, and that's this last point I'm trying to make.
And like one of the data points for that is like if you start looking at and go into it, kind of these tracking tools that we use, you can see them changing constantly.
Like every few days you can see the answer kind of take a different form, you know, citing different resources and giving a different response.
And I think, you know, our job as GrowthX is kind of to learn.

What is happening there and unpack that and make sure our customers are kind of not just getting killed by this ship to AI, but actually benefiting from it, you know, by moving faster than all the other companies that are using all the other, you know, crappy agencies that don't work the same way we do.
So, you know, how do we do that?
We can get into it.
We, and again, this is like constantly changing, but today what we do is, is one, we help them monitor kind of what the AI apps are saying about their product.
And that looks like using a combination of tools, but mainly the one here called Scrunch, where we come up with a large list of prompts to track.
We look at their responses over time, and I'll just go into one.
This is Webflow.
My client, they care about, you know, how developers think about their CMS.
I know we all struggle with, you know, in every pod, and they care, you know, are they being mentioned when people ask ChatGPT?
I mean, the answer is no for this, because they're not a headless CMS.
But what we do is we track, you know, hundreds of these prompts for each customer, and we see a few things.
We see one, are they being mentioned?
And mentioned is if their brand name pops up in the response of the AI.
We see if they're cited in the kind of articles that the AI kind of goes to Google to grab to shape its answer.
And just some deep nuances there, you know, being cited doesn't necessarily mean, you know, people are going to look at your product or be discovered.
You can see even here, you have a case of these two products.
This is an agency kind of being cited to mention, almost a competitor.
So, but what it does mean is it helps you, you know, shape the answer that's being shown.
And then lastly, you know, is the sentiment good?

Like there's a lot of cases where your product is mentioned, but it'll say something like, hey, this is a product for kind of big companies and it's expensive and a little hard to use, but, you know, it's full-featured.
So that's the first thing what we do, we kind of help our customers track.
And there's a lot of nuances to like how you pick the prompts, which I'll share a resource for, because I don't think we have time for that on this call.
Second, we kind of use these insights to shape the content that we create for them, both kind of the topics we try to cover and the way we try to write.
You know, there's a lot that goes into that.
But kind of the core of it is what you're trying to do is shape what the AI is saying about your product.
And one of the clearest ways to do that is to try to get sighted.
Yeah.
So...
Um,
So, you know, what you're able to do once you track kind of a ton of different prompts is you start seeing patterns and what is getting cited more.
You know, one of the clearest ones being, you know, if you're tracking these prompts around evaluating and discovering new products, you know, things like listicles get picked up more and more.

Like if you look at, you know, what's driving the number one suggestion, it's a listicle about best CMS platforms for 2025, which is great, you because all the AIs kind of put that into their search queries when they're trying to find an answer.
So we kind of do this across all the prompts, you know, create a list, and this becomes a part of, you know, the content backlog that we publish for them.
So, you know, one, we use it to come up with the content we create, and two, we also kind of shape the content itself to try to get cited.
You know, this is, like, super early, but, you know, what you start seeing is...
is...
You know, these AI, they do a search, it returns like 20 response, like 20, you know, pages in the, in the cert, but it only decides to use four of them.
And when it uses those four, it only decides to use like a chunk of each of those articles.
And, you know, what we want to do is unpack kind of patterns and why that is, and then help, help our customers, you know, get cited in those, in those searches.
And I'll share this link with everyone in the chat, actually, right now.
But, you know, one of the things that I'm trying to do is create a resource that everyone can reference.
And right now it's, it's this, but it'll evolve.
But in here, you'll find kind of exactly what I'm sharing now, but just in a lot more detail.
You know, the places to look is one, how we deliver.
There's a lot more nuance to like how we pick the prompts and even what we plan to do here, how we monitor, how we are.

Hoping to run audits, Dave has been really deep here as well, shaping kind of a lot of pieces of this, but mainly of how you should write and structure your website for citations and mentions, so I'd recommend everybody kind of come here to read as much of this as you can, and then this section here is, you know, like a Q&A on things we get asked from our customers.
most often, and trying to iterate this over time, so we have a good response.

Yeah, but anyway, coming back to delivery, so we, you know, we track prompts, we kind of set up monitoring and tools like Scrunch and having our Looker dashboards, we run audits, we use these insights to prioritize the content we're creating, we're kind of optimizing the content itself, and the pages they show up on, and just some examples of things we do there.
Uh,

You know, anecdotally, there have been things that have shown to help you get cited, like having kind of a little TLDR or takeaway section at the top, optimizing for kind of this concept called chunking, which is, you know, when the AI kind of cites your content, it doesn't just throw the 3000 word thing into the, you know, context and try to use that to shape an answer, it kind of like breaks it up and takes a chunk of it to drive the response.
And what that means is like, you should be a little thoughtful about how you kind of structure your content.
So if you're making a point, it doesn't kind of take the whole article to make that point.
It's, you know, concise and kind of almost segmented.
And then one of the last things that kind of dolphin in the room is, you know, for you to get mentioned, more and more, it's not enough for you to just talk about yourself.
You need to make sure actually the internet, you know, also shares like a good opinion about what you do.
And that that's public.
And a lot of what that means sometimes is, you know, other websites talk about you, like Reddits and Quora's and Linkedin's and socials.
And that's something we're kind of looking into.
And the last thing I'll leave with before I look at, I actually don't see any questions, but before I try to open it up for questions is like, this whole kind of area is just super nascent.
And you'll see a lot of people try to say like, here's the hack or here's the solution.
But the truth of it is, everyone's figuring it out.
And, you we have one Slack channel where we're trying to kind of communicate as much of this as we can.
It's D-generative engine optimization.
So I would recommend everybody join that.
And what we're going to try to do is just learn this as fast as we can and essentially help our customers kind of get ahead of this.
So, I'll leave it at that.
don't know, Bridget, Andi, anything I left out here you think it's good to talk about, but does anyone have questions?
Let me see, I think I have some all.
Bridgetta has a question in the chat.
Yeah, I see some stuff in the chat.
Listicles, yeah, I mean, I think listicles are super important.

I think what that means is, like, we need better workflows for listicles, comparison articles, alternatives, you know, like all these kind of pieces of content that, you know, if you start digging into it, it gets cited the most.

I think it goes back a little bit to kind of how we track prompts as well and what prompts we decide to track of, like, there's this kind of concept of, like, zero clicks of, I mean, think about how you kind of search and use.
on these AI apps.
So there's kind of this whole category of questions now that don't drive you to a website anymore.

Like if you're asking for a definition of something, maybe five years ago, you would have Googled that and then you would have clicked like the top link and it would have been some, you know, product where it's like tangentially helpful for them to define an answer for you, like what's sales automation or like, you know, how do you tie your shoe or something like that.
And in the past, that meant is like, you visited their website, now they know you exist, maybe you got some awareness, their product exists.
And over time, they kind of use that to shape your journey to use their product and play the long game.
But today, what that means is, you you type that to ChatGPT and that journey ends.
So a lot of that means like the effort to kind of shape user journey starts going a little bit lower into the funnel when you're researching products, you know, which is, again, why like list.
articles have been so important.
Um, does PR come in at some point?
Yeah, I, I think it definitely does.
I mean, I think, like, you can call it a bunch of different things, like this kind of concept of, like, shaping what the internet thinks about you.
You can call it PR, I think PR, you know, shapes a chunk of it.
PR shapes kind of, like, I guess the, like, publication side a little bit.
You can call it, like people, you know, like, shape what GitHub and Reddit and, you know, hack your news, think about your product.
You can call it link building.
It's like, you know, if you get more articles to mention who you are, you know, that also helps as well.

And if you exhaustively go down that list, it starts just looking like I'm doing good marketing, which is, you know, going back to that point of, like, the, like, the kind of foundational things and the fundamentals matter.
I think we'll start to roll it out gradually.
The directors all have access.
think the people who kind of shape strategy the most, I think I would like to roll out access for.
So, you know, very soon I would like the MEs to get access as well.
And for that, just ping your director.
Cool.
think that, well, I don't see any more questions.
mean, just join the Slack.
Try to just, like, roll through.
Not question, but just a comment.
Like, so AI answers are, they're super customized based on the user's past history with the AI.
And I feel like there's a great opportunity for organizations to now be hyper specific about.

The kind of customers they serve, what they do, what they don't do, because let's say a customer goes online and they're looking for a payment, like a lending company, that doesn't really care about their credit for, or something like that.
So the company's distinguishing features should be listed front and center, and maybe that's how they can come up in relevant AI related searches.
Yeah, I think being more specific to kind of the person and the use case, you know, at a high level is like very helpful.
So less of the, you know, general kind of content that tries to answer everything and more being targeted.
And I think what that means, like, you need to understand your customers more, you know, like, especially from our side, it means you got to spend more time kind of building the artifacts.
And spending time.

Jessalynn, see your question on FAQs, good, bad, different, I think FAQs are good, I think, yeah, and it's like super hard to like make that like clear connection, but, you know, going back to how these AIs like retrieve information to site, having these kind of concise blocks that have the problem and the answer kind of very tight in one, piece of text, like whether it's in the body or, you know, at the bottom in a nice little FAQ section, I think is helpful.
Maybe another thing you call out is like, at least at the moment, a lot of these kind of AI crawlers that visit websites, they don't like render the JavaScript.

So I think I've seen, you know, a few instances where clients try to get fancy with their FAQ sections, and they like animate in, and if you don't click it, the content's not there, because it doesn't, you know, kind of flash in in a nice way.
Like,
The reality is, like, the simpler, the better, like, just have it on the page is better than having it kind of dynamically load.
And Matthew, yeah, I mean, having something at the top is helpful.
Cool, I know I'm running out of time, so join the Slack, keep an eye on the Notion, because we're always going to add to it.
And then, you know, on the content of our own content for GrowthX, we've been trying to kind of publish a lot here, too.
And the first episode of the podcast that we're filming more and more of was about GEO.
So, you know, play that in the background as you're working.
I think it's a nice primer on the whole space.
Yep, and I'll share the deck in general.
Thank you, Jason.
Thanks, everyone.
Have a great weekend and see you next week.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Bi-weekly all-hands meeting to update the team on GrowthX's approach to AI-driven content strategy and optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [AI apps are reshaping how people find information and make product decisions, necessitating a shift in content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=74.0)
  - [GrowthX is helping clients adapt by monitoring AI mentions, optimizing content for citations, and shaping AI responses about their products](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
  - [The approach involves tracking prompts, running audits, prioritizing content creation, and optimizing existing content for AI consumption](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [This field is rapidly evolving; the team must stay agile and continue learning to help clients stay ahead](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)

## Topics

### Changing Landscape of Information Discovery

  - [Shift from search-driven traffic to AI app-driven information retrieval](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=74.0)
  - [Anecdotal reports of declining search traffic for some customers](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Rise in high-converting referral traffic, indicating AI apps' growing influence](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)

### GrowthX's Approach to AI-Driven Content Strategy

  - [Monitor AI app mentions of client products using tools like Scrunch](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=382.0)
  - [Track hundreds of prompts per customer to analyze mentions, citations, and sentiment](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
  - [Use insights to shape content creation and optimization strategies](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=512.0)
  - [Focus on getting cited by AI to influence responses about client products](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=534.0)

### Content Optimization Techniques

  - [Create more targeted, long-tail content to match personalized AI searches](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=256.0)
  - [Regularly refresh and update existing content to maintain relevance](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=307.0)
  - [Structure content for "chunking" - concise, segmented points for easy AI consumption](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Implement TLDR or takeaway sections at the top of content](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [Develop better workflows for high-performing content types (listicles, comparisons, alternatives)](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=560.0)

### Monitoring and Tools

  - [Use Scrunch and Looker dashboards for tracking AI mentions and citations](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [Run regular audits to assess AI performance and optimize strategy](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)
  - [Share insights and best practices through Slack channel (D-generative engine optimization)](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=818.0)
  - [Notion resource with detailed information on delivery methods, monitoring, and writing techniques](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=639.0)

### Broader Marketing Implications

  - [Shift focus to shaping internet perception of products beyond owned content](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=774.0)
  - [Consider PR, community engagement (GitHub, Reddit, Hacker News), and link building](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=791.0)
  - [Emphasize foundational marketing principles in the AI-driven landscape](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1004.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Team members to join the D-generative engine optimization Slack channel](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1061.0)
  - [Directors to provide access to Notion resource for MEs and strategy shapers](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0)
  - [Continue updating and referencing the shared Notion resource for latest insights](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1227.0)
  - [Listen to GrowthX podcast episode on GEO for additional context](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
  - [Stay agile and continue learning as the field rapidly evolves](https://fathom.video/share/m6K-81pBRfxzuXuGsxY9KLPH2RLzCmPx?tab=summary&timestamp=794.0)

action_items.description: Share Notion resource link in chat for team reference on GEO strategies
Coordinate with directors to give MEs access to GEO tools/resources
